channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: IdiomX A Multilingual Benchmark for Idiom Understanding, Retrieval, and Interpretation
item.description: arXiv:2606.02584v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Idiomatic expressions remain a persistent challenge for natural language processing because their meanings are often non-compositional, context-dependent, and difficult to align across languages. Existing idiom resources are often limited in scale, co...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, framework, reasoning, model, example | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On the Persistent Effects of Lexicality in Large Language Mod
item.description: arXiv:2606.02750v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representations extracted from large language models (LLMs) play an important role in many downstream applications. However, the structure of these representations is often influenced by lexical overlap rather than semantic content. Our understanding ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, model, LLM, summarization, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Topics as Proxies for Sociodemographics: How Conversational Context Affects LLM Answers
item.description: arXiv:2606.02776v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When large language models (LLMs) are used in high-stakes scenarios, such as legal, medical and financial advice, even a single conversation history is enough to drive differences in outcomes between users. Prior work has demonstrated that this result...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, research, model, LLM, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Translating Classical Poetry into Modern Prose
item.description: arXiv:2606.02806v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Padyam2Gadyam, a dataset for the task of poem-to-prose translation from 13th-17th Century Telugu Classical Poetry to contemporary Telugu and English prose. The dataset consists of 600 poems and their human-verified Telugu and English pros...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, model, LLM, analysis, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Visual Graph Scaffolds for Structural Reasoning in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.02673v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graphs have been used to enhance large language models (LLMs) for structured reasoning, mostly as external knowledge sources are provided to models at test time. In this paper, we take a different view: the value of graphs for LLMs lie not only in sup...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, fine-tuning, reasoning, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating Transformer and LSTM Frameworks for Prediction in Ungauged Basins
item.description: arXiv:2606.02791v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Watershed networks exhibit convergent topologies in which multiple tributaries merge into downstream channels,integrating diverse upstream hydrological processes. In ungauged basins, the absence of direct observations increases uncertainty and limits ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, framework, model, experiment, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Don't Gamble, GAMBLe: An Analytical Framework for AI-Driven Research Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.02863v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI-Driven Research Systems (ADRS) -- systems coupling LLMs with automated evaluation to discover algorithms, proofs, and designs -- are being optimized and adopted across domains, but the tools to analyze them have not kept pace. ADRS performance depe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, framework, model, LLM, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph Mamba Survival Analysis Based on Topology-Aware ordering
item.description: arXiv:2606.02602v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In computational pathology, Whole Slide Images (WSIs) survival analysis is crucial for patient prognosis assessment, but it faces multiple technical challenges. Although the Transformer captures long-range dependencies through its self-attention mecha...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, model, analysis, experiment, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Auditable Climate Risk Intelligence from Fragmented ESG Data: Deterministic Orchestration and Imbalance-Aware Learning for Scope 1-3 Validation
item.description: arXiv:2606.02604v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: ESG and climate risk data remain fragmented across heterogeneous Scope 1, Scope 2, and Scope 3 reporting environments, while conventional validation pipelines lack provenance aware auditability, hidden drift detection, and reproducibility oriented gov...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, framework, analysis, release, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BehaviorBench: Modeling Real-World User Decisions from Behavioral Traces
item.description: arXiv:2606.02798v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many decision-support settings require systems that adapt to individual users, but evaluation data for this problem remain limited. Existing benchmarks for user understanding often rely on simulated users or model-generated behavior, even though recen...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, retrieval, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Making Brain-Computer Interfaces More Secure
item.description: arXiv:2606.02597v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The development of brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) based on electroencephalograms (EEGs) has advanced significantly mainly to machine learning. Although the majority of earlier research has been on increasing classification accuracy, relatively littl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, model, experiment, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Assessing Region-Level EEG Contributions to Cognitive Workload Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2606.02598v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate and generalizable estimation of cognitive workload from electroencephalography (EEG) is critical for human-centered and safety-critical systems. Although EEG is widely used for workload assessment, the consistency of region-level EEG contribu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, framework, model, analysis, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Economy of Minds: Emerging Multi-Agent Intelligence with Economic Interactions
item.description: arXiv:2606.02859v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How can a population of agents self-orchestrate and self-adapt into stronger collective intelligence without centralized control? Inspired by Friedrich Hayek's economic theory of decentralized coordination in markets, we study this question through an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, study, research, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An Exploration of Collision-based Enemy Morphology Generation
item.description: arXiv:2606.02832v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite a great deal of prior research into Procedural Content Generation (PCG), relatively little prior work has explored generating enemies for video games. In particular, there is almost no work on generating enemy morphologies, the basic body plan...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, research, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Thinking Past the Answer: Evaluating Harmful Overthinking in Large Reasoning Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.02835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) improve performance by generating explicit intermediate reasoning traces through increased test-time compute, yet the assumption that longer reasoning is consistently beneficial remains under-examined. While recent eviden...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, reasoning, model, analysis, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
